metadata.description: Persistent record of semantic validation rounds for MAgPIE-agent documentation quality tracking

schema_version_history.change: added top-level regression_questions array for stability tracking across rounds; rubric hoisted to audit/flywheel_rubric.md
added G3 (magpie4 source-of-truth / version-pin discipline) and G4 (magpie4 getReport dispatch structure) regression questions; rubric bumped to v1.1

added (a) 'doc_error_answerer_beat_it' as a bugs_by_root_cause value + an optional per-question doc_errors_latent[] for LATENT doc bugs (answer correct but relied on a doc claim wrong vs code); recorded + fixed regardless of answer score - closes the G2 R22->R23->R26 regression blind spot. (b) summary.doc_quality_mean + n_questions_doc_quality + doc_quality_mean_method: doc-quality mean excluding pure answerer_confabulation questions, reported alongside the raw mean_score (mean_score_trend stays raw). See flywheel_rubric.md sec 1.5 + sec 4 and validate-semantic.md Steps 3/5/5b.

added round 'type' doc_centric_audit + per-round 'doc_centric_audit[]' block (full-doc adversarial audits, hybrid R30 and doc-centric R32); 'cap_event' on rate-limited rounds. From the 2026-05-29/30 autonomous 3-round doc-improvement push (R30 hybrid, R31 hybrid-partial-capped, R32 doc-centric).

Added round type 'instrument_integrity_partial' (R57): audits the CHECKERS, not the docs. Carries w0a_mutation_test / w0a_rolemap_validation / w1_taxonomy_reconciliation blocks and an r57_w0a_annotation field on prior rounds whose headline depended on an unasserted instrument. Declared version had been static at 1.4 since 2026-05-30 while R40-R56 kept inventing keys; this bump acknowledges the drift rather than continuing it.

metadata.instructions: Append new rounds to the 'rounds' array. Each round must include all fields. Bug sources: 'doc_error' = bug in the AI documentation, 'answerer_confabulation' = agent invented wrong info despite docs being correct, 'ambiguous' = unclear which caused the error. Each new round MUST include at least 1 regression question from the top-level 'regression_questions' array (added 2026-05-23); score against expected_answer_summary; if regression, set drift_observed=true and update used_in_rounds. Severity tiers + audit format: audit/flywheel_rubric.md.
metadata.root_cause_vocabulary_note: bugs_by_root_cause keys: doc_error (doc wrong AND answer wrong); doc_error_answerer_beat_it (doc wrong but answer right - LATENT, fix anyway); answerer_confabulation (doc correct, answer wrong); answerer_style_or_framing; process_gap; ambiguous. See flywheel_rubric.md sec 1.5.

regression_questions.question: What is the default realization of module 14 (yields)? List the equations defined in its equations.gms.
regression_questions.expected_answer_summary: Default realization: managementcalib_aug19 (verify via 'grep cfg$gms$yields ../config/default.cfg'). Equations defined in equations.gms: exactly 2 — q14_yield_crop (crop yields) and q14_yield_past (pasture yields). Verify exact line ranges against modules/14_yields/managementcalib_aug19/equations.gms. (Note: 'q14_yieldcalib' was historically listed in this anchor but does NOT exist as a defined equation — corrected 2026-05-24 R22 after audit.)
regression_questions.calibration_intent: tests whether the agent (a) consults default.cfg before describing the realization, (b) reads the actual equations file rather than reconstructing from memory, (c) reports the equation count correctly (Bug_Taxonomy.md Pattern 6 -- Hardcoded counts drift)

regression_questions.question: Walk through how vm_carbon_stock is computed in Module 52 and where it enters the GHG-policy cost in Module 56. Cite the relevant equations and file:line locations.
regression_questions.expected_answer_summary: vm_carbon_stock is DECLARED in Module 56 (price_aug22/declarations.gms:34) — NOT in Module 52. Land modules (29 cropland, 31 pasture, 32 forestry, 34 urban, 35 natveg) and Module 59 (SOM, soilc pool) POPULATE the variable. Module 52 only READS vm_carbon_stock and pcm_carbon_stock to compute CO2 emissions via q52_emis_co2_actual (normal_dec17/equations.gms:16-19). Module 56's chain: q56_emis_pricing_co2 (equations.gms:19-22) reads (pcm - vm)/timestep_length → v56_emis_pricing → q56_emission_cost_oneoff (equations.gms:45-52) → v56_emission_cost → q56_emission_costs (equations.gms:56-58) → vm_emission_costs(i). The %c56_carbon_stock_pricing% switch default is 'actualNoAcEst'. Citations must use full relative paths per MANDATE 16. (Corrected 2026-05-24 R22 — earlier summary incorrectly attributed declaration to M52.)
regression_questions.calibration_intent: tests (a) cross-module dependency tracing, (b) precise file:line citations, (c) variable-name fidelity (no confabulation of related-sounding names like vm_carbon_stocks), (d) gate-tabulation behavior when the chain has multiple branches, (e) producer-vs-consumer distinction (which module DECLARES vs which only READS the variable)
regression_questions.r26_note: Drifted at R26 (populator-list recurrence). Doc root-cause fixed in R26 (module_56.md + module_52.md -> 29/31/32/34/35/59); expect recovery next re-measure.
regression_questions.r27_note: Recovered to 9.0 at R27 after the R26 doc fix; drift cleared.

regression_questions.question: Which version of magpie4 does this agent's source-of-truth clone reflect, and how was that version determined? Cite the file(s) you read.
regression_questions.expected_answer_summary: Agent must read project/version_pins.json (NOT the workspace clone at ~/Documents/Work/Workspace/magpie4/). Reports the version and SHA from that file; identifies the upstream authoritative source as ../input/renv.lock (Packages.magpie4 entry — fields: Version + RemoteSha). Verification at audit time: read project/version_pins.json directly — the agent's claimed version, SHA (or 10-char prefix), and 'resolution' field must match. The workspace clone is intentionally NOT the source-of-truth (it drifts ahead of the renv pin; as of 2026-05-24 it is v2.75.1 while the renv pin is v2.70.0). The pin can be regenerated via 'python3 scripts/sync_magpie4_clone.py'. Critical-severity confabulation: claiming a different version than version_pins.json shows; citing the workspace clone as source-of-truth; fabricating a SHA. Citations must use full relative paths per MANDATE 16. (Added 2026-05-24 alongside the magpie4 lean-scaffolding initiative.)
ground_truth_sources: project/version_pins.json (cached snapshot of the pin; regenerated by scripts/sync_magpie4_clone.py)
regression_questions.calibration_intent: tests (a) source-of-truth discipline for the two-clone setup (SHA-pinned .cache/sources/magpie4/ vs drift-prone ~/Documents/Work/Workspace/magpie4/), (b) reading the auto-loaded agent/helpers/magpie4_reference.md helper before answering (the three pre-answer rules), (c) version-pin awareness (not confabulating a version from training data)

regression_questions.question: How does magpie4::getReport organize its reporting? Describe the dispatch pattern, how many unique report* functions it calls, and cite the file:line range from the pinned clone.
regression_questions.expected_answer_summary: Agent must read from .cache/sources/magpie4/R/getReport.R (the SHA-pinned clone), NOT from the workspace clone. Dispatch is a flat tryList(...) of unconditional calls to ~106 unique report* functions (117 total call lines — some functions called multiple times with different argument combos, e.g., reportYields with physical=TRUE/FALSE, reportProcessing with two indicator values, reportPriceFoodIndex with two baseyears). NOT an 'if (any(grepl(...)))' or control-argument dispatch — there is NO 'control' argument; the function signature is getReport(gdx, file=NULL, scenario=NULL, filter=c(1,2,7), detail=TRUE, level='regglo', ...). tryList catches per-function failures so one broken report* does not crash the whole report. Body lives at .cache/sources/magpie4/R/getReport.R lines 56-235 (v2.70.0 @ a360d8c9ec); the tryList block is approximately lines 62-181. Major-severity bug to catch: confabulating a control-arg-gated dispatch (this was the magpie4 scaffolding plan's incorrect initial description; the helper documents the corrected structure). Critical if the agent claims a different unique-function count by an order of magnitude (e.g., 'thousands' or '20'). (Added 2026-05-24 alongside the magpie4 lean-scaffolding initiative.)
regression_questions.calibration_intent: tests (a) reading from the SHA-pinned clone rather than the workspace clone, (b) faithful description of the actual dispatch (no confabulation of control-based gating or grepl filtering), (c) reasonable count of unique report* functions (target ~106; off-by-few is Minor, off-by-order is Critical), (d) precise file:line citation with version pin per MANDATE 16

Q1-B9, Q1-B10, Q2-B7, Q2-B8, Q3-B4, Q3-B5 (Minor/Nitpick, oversimplifications): Shared C_max for plantation bisection; 'fixed LPJmL' for primforest; pre-update residue formula simplification; stacking framing; start.gms:17-38 imprecise range; inferred 'no FRA target' rationale. All defensible; worth documenting in a follow-up pass if user wants.
key_findings: Mean score 8.17/10 — within 'Mostly Accurate' band. All three questions scored 7.5-8.5, indicating consistent quality after the 2026-04-20 sync.
Zero Critical or Fundamentally Flawed answers. No fabricated variables, no wrong equation names, no wrong realizations.

Dominant bug class: file:line citation drift (13/23 = 57%). This matches the historic pattern (71% in Bug_Taxonomy). Likely caused by drafting docs against one snapshot then not re-verifying citations after minor commits restructured the code. Mitigation: for citations in post-sync doc updates, derive lines from the final merged origin/develop, not from an intermediate commit.

One Major doc_error: M52 uncalib consumer list missed M32 afforestation/NDC. Rationale: my sync focused on the M29 tree-cover addition and didn't audit which OTHER modules had always consumed uncalib variants. Mitigation: when adding '_uncalib' parameters, grep the whole codebase for all consumers (grep `-uncalib` across modules/**/*.gms).
Answerer confabulation rate: 30% of bugs (7/23) — well within the 22-33% range of recent targeted rounds. The answerer reliably drew from the docs but introduced minor imprecisions.

Validation pipeline improvement: the first round of the syntactic validator post-sync flagged 27 GAMS variable mismatches, all expected (new variables on develop not yet in local tree). After git pull origin develop, 10 remained — all historical references in 'formerly X' context, which the checker couldn't distinguish. Resolved by switching backtick wrappers to italics for deprecated names. This is a reusable pattern for future sync cycles.

rounds.scope: Post-sync targeted validation of the 6 modules updated in the 2026-05-16 develop sync (PR #866 trade, #871 nlp_ipopt, #876 secdforest carbon)

questions.question: After PR #866's bilateral-trade overhaul, name the three trade-cost interface variables and their defining Module 21 equations, explain what each represents, explain how Module 11 aggregates them, and name the former single variable and the default trade realization.
questions.notes: All substantive claims correct: 3 variables (vm_cost_trade_tariff/margin/feasibility), 3 equations, formulas, M11 aggregation, former vm_cost_trade, selfsuff_reduced default, defaults s21_cost_import=1500 and s21_min_trade_margin_forestry=62. Q1-B1 (Major, answerer_confabulation): answer framed PR #866 as a clean rename of an equation pair, understating that one combined per-commodity cost stream was split into three and the objective-facing constraint changed from =e= to =g=. Q1-B2 (Minor): v11_cost_reg/vm_cost_glo objective path not spelled out. Q1-B3 (Nitpick): 'sums 32 cost terms' loose since vm_reward_cdr_aff is subtracted.

questions.question: Is nlp_ipopt the default optimization realization? What solver does it use vs the default, how does it handle option files, and what is its retry/re-solve behavior?
questions.notes: Correct: nlp_ipopt non-default (default nlp_apr17), Ipopt vs CONOPT4, dynamic two-file put/putclose, ipopt.op2 written-but-never-activated, no s80_resolve_option cycling, scalar inventory. Q2-B1 (Major, doc_clarity): answer claimed nlp_apr17 does NOT write option files dynamically and uses a static conopt4.opt. False: nlp_apr17 also writes conopt4.opt via put/putclose; the real difference is conopt4.op2 via compile-time $onecho. module_80.md's 'Unlike the CONOPT-based realizations...' framing invited the misread; fixed this session. Q2-B2/B3 (Minor): retry-loop and default.cfg comment-block line ranges slightly off.

questions.question: How does Module 35 track natural-origin secondary forest for carbon density (parameters, blend formula, the equation and its density argument)? In Module 52, how does the growing-stock bisection determine Chapman-Richards k and what bounds it?
questions.notes: All load-bearing claims correct: p35_secdforest_natural / pc35_secdforest_natural / p35_carbon_density_secdforest, the blend formula, q35_carbon_secdforest density argument, the 25-iteration bisection, s52_k_high_secdf=0.1 and s52_k_high_plant=0.15, hardcoded 0.001 lower bound, replaced-0.3 history. Q3-B2 (Minor)/Q3-B3 (Nitpick): two citation ranges start on comment lines. Q3-B4 (Nitpick): 'FRA 2025 target' over-specifies the parameter name. Key missing nuance (added to module_52.md): the bisection has no convergence test, so k clamps to a bound if the FRA target is unreachable.

bugs_fixed_this_session: Q2-B1 (Major, doc_clarity): rewrote module_80.md's CONOPT-vs-Ipopt option-file contrast and the realization-comparison table row. CONOPT realizations also write conopt4.opt via put/putclose; the real difference is conopt4.op2 ($onecho) vs ipopt.op2 (put).
Q1-B1 (Major): reworded module_21.md's equation note to say PR #866 SPLIT one combined trade-cost stream into three, not a clean rename of an equation pair.
module_52.md: removed a confusing 'lower bound s52_k_high_secdf' phrase (it is an UPPER bound) and added a 'no convergence test' caveat to the bisection section (the Q3 missing nuance).
bugs_deferred: Q1-B2/B3, Q2-B2/B3, Q3-B2/B3/B4 (Minor/Nitpick): objective-path incompleteness, citation ranges that begin on comment lines, and 'FRA 2025' naming. Low impact, mostly answerer-side imprecision. Recorded, not separately fixed.
key_findings: Mean 8.0/10, 'Mostly Accurate' band, consistent with the post-sync R20 result (8.17). Zero Critical bugs; no fabricated variables, equations, or realizations in any of the three answers.

Both Major bugs were contrast/causality framing errors, not factual fabrication: the answerer compressed a 'split into three' into a 'rename', and over-generalized a doc's 'static $onecho for .op2' into 'static option files'. The two doc passages that invited those misreads were tightened.
First round whose docs validate 33/33 with the hardened tooling (the validator set -e crash and the equation-checker italics-blindness were both found and fixed during the preceding sync).

Q3 surfaced a genuine model behavior worth documenting: Module 52's growing-stock bisection is a fixed 25-iteration loop with no convergence test, so k clamps to a bound when the FRA target is unreachable. Caveat added to module_52.md.

questions.notes: Q1-B1 (Major, doc_error propagation): Sonnet faithfully reproduced module_17.md's stale claim that 'vm_prod(j,k) for livestock remains zero' / 'vm_prod_reg(i, livst_rum) sums to zero'. This was true pre-M71 foragebased_jul23 (default since 2023) but is now wrong — M71's q71_feed_forage and q71_prod_mon_liv constrain vm_prod(j, kli_rum) and vm_prod(j, kli_mon), so vm_prod_reg(i, kli) is non-zero in default config. The source comment at modules/17_production/flexreg_apr16/realization.gms:13-14 ('For the time being, this approach is not applied to livestock products') is the original confabulation seed; it was not updated when M71 foragebased_jul23 landed. FIXED THIS SESSION: module_17.md §1.3, §7.2, §10.5 rewritten to acknowledge M71 foragebased_jul23 behavior and flag the stale realization.gms comment. USD17MER/yr'); the GAMS declaration at declarations.gms:10 says just 'mio USD17MER' (no /yr), and module_44.md L94, L235 match the GAMS comment. CONFOUND: 6 catches were aided by internal inconsistency (sibling statement left un-edited); the cleanest internally-consistent injection (carbon_balance) was the one missed, so 86% is an UPPER BOUND on code-only find-rate. Demonstrates find-rate < verify-rate; residual concentrates in deep-Layer-2 x not-pointed-at. See PHASE_B_findrate.md.
summary.phase_c_coverage_map: Doc correctness is a 3-layer stack: Layer 1 (mechanical checkers, 28 checks) DETERMINISTICALLY covers identifier-existence/default-label/citation/count - and several Phase-0 planted classes (fabricated names, wrong defaults) are caught here too (LLM catch redundant). Layer 2 (mechanism, causal-direction, producer-vs-consumer identity, default-state caveat, solution-level reads, module attribution, prose signature) is LLM-ONLY = the genuine H1/H2 surface. Layer 3: doc-level LLM coverage COMPLETE (46/46 module + 33 non-module); residual = CLAIM-level coverage within docs. See PHASE_C_coverage_map.md.
summary.final_verdict: Auditor blind spot is NARROW: causal/data-flow-direction claims needing non-local inference (class B), in long docs, without a forced both-endpoints check. Per-claim=100%, locally-checkable-deep=caught, mechanical-layer=deterministic. Real corpus clean on direction (3-agent ensemble). H1 holds broadly; residual = class-B capability gap, mitigable by (a) a standing both-endpoints audit practice (Opus, today) and (b) Fable/Mythos for class-B-in-long-docs + unknown-unknowns. Unknown-unknown class still untestable by same-gen fixtures.
summary.followup1_deep_findrate: Find-rate on DEEP single-statement Layer-2 injections: 2/3 found. carbon_balance class-B causal (soilc serial-vs-parallel) MISSED AGAIN (2nd time, clean, thorough 21-file audit -> verdict CLEAN). Module_Dependencies class-D default-state CAUGHT clean (one solve.gms grep). Data_Flow class-C producer caught but injection had a residual self-contradiction (not a clean data point). Sharpened residual: the blind spot is specifically causal/data-flow-DIRECTION claims requiring NON-LOCAL multi-module inference to falsify; locally-checkable deep claims are caught. See FOLLOWUP_RESULTS.md.
summary.followup2_real_direction_audit: 3 independent adversarial agents (one per doc: carbon_balance, Data_Flow, Module_Dependencies), each forced to do the both-endpoints non-local check. NO genuine direction errors in the real docs - the known-trap soilc M52/M56 arrow is correctly documented as PARALLEL (carbon_balance:615); 14->30->17 genuinely serial. 2 borderline minor imprecisions surfaced (Module_Dependencies:198 56->32 framing; :212 indirect-as-direct 70->14 dependency) - recorded, not auto-fixed. So the injection-test residual is a CAPABILITY gap, not a live bug. Method lesson: a single same-tier null is low-confidence on a blind-spot class; ensemble-with-forced-check is the trustworthy mitigation (runs on Opus).
summary.item3_tighter_findrate_and_mandate21_val: UNMITIGATED tighter run (5 internally-consistent single-statement injections): class-B (causal/data-flow direction) 0/3, class-C (producer) 2/2 -> combined class-B = 0/4 (0%), class-C = 3/3 across the session. The fully-internally-consistent class-C producer injection was STILL caught, resolving the prior internal-inconsistency confound (class C is caught by a declarations.gms grep, not self-contradiction). MITIGATED re-run (MANDATE 21 both-endpoints instruction added to the prompt): the 2 inter-module VARIABLE-arrow class-B bugs recovered from MISSED to CAUGHT (2/2) - the fix is VALIDATED for the dominant code-grounded form; the 1 ABSTRACT causal-chain bug (Prices->costs->total) was still missed (no variable endpoints to grep -> added an input-vs-derived note to MANDATE 21). Side-finding: the run surfaced + fixed 3 real input-filename bugs in Data_Flow.md s1.2. See ITEM3_findrate_and_mitigation.md.

questions.topic: M14/M35 - which growing stock does youngsecdf wood production use, and does it matter for carbon?
questions.verdict: Answer accurate and complete; DOC had a Major over-claim that the answer faithfully inherited.
doc_defects: MAJOR (self-inflicted, same session): module_35.md/module_14.md/carbon_balance_conservation.md asserted a UNIVERSAL direction for the pre-fix bias ('booked almost no carbon', 'evade land-CO2 caps'). Both curves share an asymptote, so calibrated-vs-uncalibrated reduces to the growth-rate k, and the FRA calibration RAISES k in some regions and LOWERS it in others (M52 input.gms:47 says FRA stock is below LPJmL potential 'in most regions', not all). Where k is lowered the pre-fix yield was too LOW (possibly clamped to 0 by s14_minimum_growing_stock) and the fix INCREASES youngsecdf wood supply. Fixed: mechanism now attributed to the commit author with an explicit regional-direction caveat.
MINOR (MANDATE 18): module_35.md:127 omitted Module 14 -- the consumer added by the very commit being documented -- from the pm_carbon_density_secdforest_ac_uncalib consumer list. Fixed.
leads_not_asserted: Auditor's algebraic claim that an analogous yield/carbon curve mismatch is STILL LIVE for secdforest proper (q35_prod_secdforest reads the calibrated curve; q35_carbon_secdforest reads a natural-origin-weighted blend, pot_forest_may24/presolve.gms:248-252). Recorded in BACKLOG as an unverified lead with an explicit gate (reproduce in a run) -- deliberately NOT written into the module docs as fact.
Auditor's claim that module_35.md:305's rationale for the natural-origin feature has the calibration direction backwards. Likewise unverified, likewise BACKLOG-only.

questions.topic: M32 - can I just set c32_aff_policy = ndcdelay and trust the run?
questions.verdict: CRITICAL doc defect. The answerer did everything right -- reproduced the doc faithfully, cited it, and even told the user to verify empirically -- and was wrong anyway, because the doc was a false bug report.
doc_defects: CRITICAL (self-inflicted, same session): module_32.md + the new module_32_notes.md claimed c32_aff_policy = ndcdelay (and affexp) SILENTLY produces a zero afforestation policy, and the BACKLOG queued 'report to the PRISMA authors?'. FALSE. Every premise was true (no GAMS code branch; the on-disk .cs3 really has only none,npi,ndc; affexp/ndcdelay appear 0x in its 6405 lines; neither feature commit bumped cfg$input) but the CONCLUSION was a false inference: npi_ndc_aff_pol.cs3 is gitignored (.gitignore:7) and REGENERATED at run start -- scripts/start_functions.R:380-391 detects the missing column (affexp_missing/ndcdelay_missing) and forces calc_NPI_NDC() to rewrite it, under the DEFAULT cfg$recalc_npi_ndc <- 'ifneeded' (config/default.cfg:123). The guards were added BY THE VERY COMMITS being accused (a54cd02c6, 58bde5788). Both policies are wired into named presets (config/scenario_config.csv:51). RETRACTED and rewritten; the residual true hazard (cfg$recalc_npi_ndc <- FALSE skips regeneration; GAMS zero-fills an absent table column silently) is now scoped correctly.
questions.root_cause: The agent verified the GAMS layer exhaustively and then inferred the provenance of a GENERATED artifact from modules/*/input/ contents plus git history alone. It never grepped the R start layer. A single `grep -rn "c32_aff_policy" .` would have surfaced scripts/start_functions.R:380. NEW RULE (recorded in module_32_notes.md + BACKLOG): a file under modules/*/input/ is not necessarily an input you were given -- it may be a run-time product of scripts/. Before claiming an input is missing or stale, grep scripts/ for the switch name AND the file name.

rounds.summary: The highest-value round in the campaign's recent history, and the clearest possible justification for keeping semantic validation drift-triggered rather than retiring it. Both probes targeted doc content written HOURS earlier by this same agent during the develop sync -- i.e. the round audited the auditor. Result: 1 CRITICAL and 1 MAJOR self-inflicted doc defect, both of which had passed the full 42-check syntactic validator (0 errors) and both of which the docs-only answerers reproduced perfectly and confidently. The Critical one was a FALSE BUG REPORT against a colleague's just-merged PRISMA feature that was one step from being sent upstream. Doc-quality mean 4.5 is the lowest since R30 and is entirely self-inflicted -- which is the point: a green validator, a fluent answer, and a confident agent are jointly compatible with a doc that is simply wrong, and only code-grounded adversarial audit closes that gap. Answer quality was high throughout (9.0): the answerers were not the failure point. Also of note: both answerers spontaneously surfaced real doc gaps unprompted (a stale 'Last Verified' footer; the s32_npi_ndc_reversal-vs-ndcdelay naming collision), which is evidence the docs-only answering discipline is working as designed.

per_doc.headline: MY OWN R53 RETRACTION WAS OVER-CORRECTED. It claimed the .cs3 is 'a generated artifact, not an input you download'; verified it is BOTH -- a downloaded all-zero DUMMY (header 'Dummy file for NPI/INDC policies'; manifest files:5; 6400 rows 0 nonzero) that is regenerated at run start. The real footgun is BROADER than I wrote: with recalc_npi_ndc<-FALSE, EVERY policy silently zeroes incl. the DEFAULT npi (c32_aff_policy defaults to 'npi', not 'none').

Module 58 (peatland, default realization v2) was ABSENT from the cross-module graph; 4 M32 interfaces (vm_land_forestry, vm_landexpansion_forestry, vm_landreduction_forestry, pcm_land_forestry) mis-attributed to M10/M35 when M58 is their sole external consumer (M10 references none). Plus M12 missing as a source, mass-vs-volume unit error, unweighted-vs-weighted rotation mean, inverted circular-dep ordering. Retraction text itself verified CLEAN.

Two Criticals INDEPENDENTLY REPLICATED by the blind positive-control auditor: fm_croparea attributed to M10 but declared in M30 (simple_apr24/input.gms:71, default); and a fabricated M14<->M17/M55/M59 circular-dependency chain (S21.3) whose arrows do not exist in code. The 2026-07-14 im_growing_stock_ysf section verified CORRECT (sole consumer q35_prod_other; clamps/sets/citations exact).

vm_landexpansion listed as an M35 OUTPUT to M10; it is DECLARED and DEFINED in M10, M35 only reads it (MANDATE 21 inverted data-flow). 2 count-drift Majors (4-vs-5 disturbance modes; '15 age-classes' vs the 62-member ac set). Both leads refuted/deferred; line 305 left untouched.

'Peatland Carbon Not Modeled' was FALSE -- M58/v2 (default) models it (q58_peatland_emis -> vm_emissions_reg(i2,'peatland',...)). Plus phantom residue-burn CH4 MACC, N2O MACC misattributed to M51 (it is M50, transitive), invented pm_timestep_length GDX read (it is a compile-time macro m_timestep_length), default-ON FRA calibration omitted.

rounds.doc_quality_mean_method: Scored on the DOC (Stage 2 direct doc-vs-code audit), NOT the Stage 3 answer. Batch-1 mean of per-doc quality is low (~4.8/10) because every one of the 5 freshly-written docs carried >=1 Critical or a cluster of Majors -- consistent with R53's finding of a high defect rate in same-session content. Stage 3's 10.0 answer-quality mean is on the CORRECTED docs and is the regression proof that the fixes convey correctly; the two numbers measure different things (pre-fix doc state vs post-fix answer state) and must not be conflated.

leads_disposition.lead_A: Structural half CONFIRMED from code by both module_35 and carbon_balance auditors (different curves do feed q35_prod_secdforest yield vs q35_carbon_secdforest carbon). Materiality DELIBERATELY NOT ADJUDICATED (needs a model run); unchecked layers named (pcm_carbon_stock postsolve carry-forward, M56 pricing scope f56_emis_policy.csv, magpie4 reporting); settling experiment stated. Neither doc asserts anything false -> no edit. The decidability split held exactly as designed.
leads_disposition.lead_B: UNRESOLVED. The M52 bisection inputs (f52_fra_nrf_gs.cs4, f52_growth_par.csv, lpj_carbon_stocks.cs3) are gitignored R-layer products ABSENT from the repo, so the prior auditor's 'calibration raises k in 10 of 12 regions' figure is NOT reproducible here. That figure had driven an R53 Major correction; it should now be treated as UNVERIFIED. Line 305 left untouched in either direction.

rounds.summary: R54 verified the 2026-07-14 sync's 5 highest-risk docs (Batch 1 of 10) and settled the 2 R53 leads. Positive control PASSED. Stage 2 found + fixed 73 confirmed bugs incl. 7 Criticals; gate clean. Two epistemic headlines: (1) MY OWN R53 retraction of the ndcdelay claim was itself over-corrected in the opposite direction -- the .cs3 is both a downloaded dummy AND a run-time artifact, and the real footgun is broader (recalc<-FALSE zeroes the default npi too); the producer rule injected into the engine this morning is what caught it. (2) Module 58 (peatland, default realization) was entirely absent from the cross-module doc graph, producing a confident false-negative 'peatland not modeled' plus 4 mis-attributed M32 interfaces. Two module_14 Criticals were independently replicated by the blind control auditor (fm_croparea M10->M30; a fabricated M14 dependency cycle). Stage 3 answer-quality on the CORRECTED docs: 6/6 anchors at 10.0, zero drift -- the retraction demonstrably took. Lead A structural-only (materiality refused, needs a run); Lead B unresolved (calibration inputs absent from repo; the 'raises k in 10/12' figure is not reproducible and is downgraded to unverified). Batch 2 + a possible model run to settle Lead A remain open.

stage2_doc_audit.confidence_caveat: The 1 Critical + 7 Major SURVIVED adversarial refutation. The 19 Minor + 1 Info were auditor-confirmed but NOT independently refuted (by design: only Crit/Major are refuted) -> weaker evidence.

Independently re-derived by the orchestrator (14_yields/managementcalib_aug19/presolve.gms:66; 35_natveg/pot_forest_may24/presolve.gms:117,242,251). ~9 months old; survived the 2026-06-29 wide-net 9.52 pass.

rounds.answer_quality: NOT MEASURED. No answerers ran this round. R55 is a DOC-quality (doc-vs-code) measurement. The residual density below MUST NOT be relayed as, or alongside, an answer-quality score (the 9.52 misreading is exactly what this round exists to correct).

residual_density_per_100_claims._headline: Every ALREADY-MECHANIZED class came back clean or near-clean (attribution_declare 0/22 via Check 31; default_value 0/36 via Check 20; realization 0/3 via Checks 16/18; citation 1.12/100 via the citation checker). ALL residual sits in UNMECHANIZED classes. All 12 attribution bugs are stale OMISSIONS, zero phantoms -> maintenance lag, not confabulation: docs track declarations reliably and lose track of readers.

rounds.measurement_verdict: TARGETED_ONLY (not a full-corpus depth campaign). (a) >=1 Critical attribution omission per hub: 1 of 3 -> FAIL. (b1) 0.33 Criticals/doc vs 0.50 bar -> FAIL. (b2) 2.27 vs ~2 Major-attribution per 100 -> a threshold-straddler on a 2-EVENT numerator (Poisson 95% CI ~[0.24,7.22]); not measurable at this sample size, and explicitly NOT relayed as a bare label. The rule's premise was verified rather than assumed: hubs are genuinely top-centrality (27.0 reader-edges/doc vs 7.3 rest; ranks #1/#3/#7 of 42), so an upper-bound sample failing 2 of 3 criteria predicts a corpus below threshold. Projection ~2-3.5 Criticals across the 39 remaining docs (~1 per 11-19) vs a bar of 1 per 2.
rounds.followups: R56 candidate: the depth audit's ENTIRE attribution yield (12 bugs) was mechanically derivable from the role map -- a recurring role-map-vs-doc consumer-set diff catches the class at ~zero marginal cost with ZERO LLM passes. Broadening that diff (var-block '**Consumers**:' fields + Oxford lists) already reproduces the R55 Critical in milliseconds AND found 2 omissions the 2.73M-token audit MISSED (module_52.md:443 M57 [later shown to be a slice-level FP, allowlisted] and module_58.md:391 M57 [REAL; module_58 was outside the audit scope]). CORRECTION recorded: data_flow_direction is NOT cheaply mechanizable -- both its Majors are slice-disjointness (M52 does populate vm_emissions_reg, just a disjoint slice), which needs GAMS index-set reasoning, not a role-map lookup.
rounds.summary: Depth-first probe of the 'doc accuracy is solved (9.52)' premise on the 3 highest-centrality hubs. 498 claims -> 28 confirmed findings (1 Critical + 7 Major refuted-and-survived). The premise does not hold at depth: 9.52 measured 'few bugs found at that depth/FNR', not 'few bugs exist'. But the corpus-wide verdict is TARGETED_ONLY, not a full campaign -- and the highest-leverage move is mechanization, not more LLM depth: every mechanized class is clean, and the audit's whole attribution yield was reproducible by a deterministic diff.
rounds.r57_w0a_annotation: R57/W0a ANNOTATION (2026-07-16, added per user decision 'annotate now, don't retract'): this round's headline was measured by an instrument whose ground-truth extractor had NO automated assertion. W0a mutation-tested the 24 rostered checkers: build_role_map/build_slice_role_map, build_producer_map, build_gams_index, build_canonical_map and every load_allowlist can be replaced with `raise` while the entire self-test battery stays green (67/164 functions = 41% dead-to-test; 50.3% mutation survival within detection logic). The self-tests cover the DIFFING half but never the map-BUILDING half -- they run in 30ms from a scratch copy with NO MAgPIE tree because they never read it. The only validation of the map builders was one-time manual historical replays, which are NOT automated and NOT gated (validate_consistency.sh references them only in comments; the battery never sets MAGPIE_DIR). CRUCIALLY, R57 then VALIDATED the role map empirically rather than leaving this as doubt: an independent re-derivation (own comment-stripper, own tokenizer, nothing imported from the module under test) found ZERO under-populated (module,var) pairs out of 438. So this round's 0.00 STANDS. One variable was dropped entirely -- vm_AEI, declared M41, consumed M30 -- because CROSS_IFACE_RE required a lowercase letter after the prefix; fixed at both duplicate sites, and the docs' vm_AEI claims were themselves CORRECT, so this was a coverage hole with zero doc-bug consequence. Permanent guard now installed: scripts/check_rolemap_completeness.py. The instrument was sound but UNGUARDED -- the measurement was lucky, not protected. It is protected now.

rounds.positive_control: PASSED. built.gams_slices.py: GAMS index-set resolution. 250 sets from core/sets.gms + every modules/*/*/sets.gms (2 same-name-across-realizations collisions EXPOSED, not silently resolved). slices_intersect -> True|False|None; DISJOINT only from a both-resolved non-overlapping dimension -> non-regressive by construction.
built.check_dependent_direction.py: Check 36 (advisory, WIRED). Mechanizes data_flow_direction (R55: 6.38/100; R51 MANDATE-21 parallel-not-serial). Flags a documented hand-off whose write-slice and read-slice are PROVABLY disjoint.
built.check_bindability.py: Check 37 (advisory, NOT wired - a to-do list would be gate noise). Flags attribution claims the mechanical layer cannot see (module named, no interface var in scope). Output is a COVERAGE/TO-DO list, never a defect list.
built.check_attribution_omissions.py: slice logic wired into diff_doc; the module_52 57:vm_emissions_reg allowlist row RETIRED (suppression no longer load-bearing).

corrections_on_record: R55 claimed data_flow_direction was NOT cheaply mechanizable and should stay LLM territory. WRONG once the slice layer exists: Check 36 reproduces BOTH its Majors from real pre-fix git text. The 'needs a real build' half of that call was right; the 'not worth it' half was not.

The orchestrator's stated precision rule ('any UNRESOLVED dimension -> never DISJOINT') was over-strict and would have FAILED control A. One provably-disjoint dimension is dispositive regardless of other dims (a Cartesian product is empty as soon as one axis fails to overlap). The implementation is sounder than the spec.
silent_green_bugs_found: check_attribution_omissions --self-test reported '19 cases / PASS' while only 16 assertions RAN: the `for case in cases:` loop sat ABOVE three appended cases, so the var-block and Oxford regression locks were INERT. Loop relocated; 21 assertions now execute. Found by a subagent, not by the harness -- the sentinel discipline (SELFTEST_OK) proves a check ran, NOT that its assertions did.

gams_slices set parser: the / ... / delimiter regex required whitespace on BOTH sides, which real GAMS omits ('/underaged,working,retired /'). Block tracking desynced and FABRICATED a set named 'others' whose members were English prose. 192 -> 250 sets after the fix.

rounds.scope: Class-discovery round. Premise: every taxonomy here is self-generated from what an instrument already surfaced, so the found-bug corpus is CENSORED by the detection apparatus and mining labels can only resurface classes already named.

w0a_mutation_test.method: AST mutation of each rostered checker's logic (self_test bodies excluded); a mutation that SURVIVES means no assertion covers that path. Harness carries its own positive/negative control pair (kills a covered mutant, reports a dead-code mutant as surviving).
w0a_mutation_test.pattern: every ground-truth extractor is dead to test; 8 checkers score 0% dead (gams_slices, check_gams_citations_impl, check_hedged_claims, check_gams_equations, check_gams_realizations, check_no_bare_cites, check_doc_var_existence) -- NOT systemic rot, one specific structural seam.

w0a_rolemap_validation.verdict: ROLE MAP SOUND -- R55/R56 largely VINDICATED, not falsified
w0a_rolemap_validation.root_cause: CROSS_IFACE_RE = ^(?:vm|pm|im|pcm|fm)_[a-z] required lowercase after the prefix; contradicted is_interface_var(), which resolves via the case-insensitive GAMS_INTERFACE_RE and returns True for vm_AEI. Constant was DUPLICATED across check_attribution_omissions.py and check_attribution_prose.py.
w0a_rolemap_validation.doc_bug_consequence: NONE -- the docs' vm_AEI claims were correct. Coverage hole, not a doc error.
w0a_rolemap_validation.control_cycle: detector shipped BEFORE fix: red (138 vars, dropped=1, rc=1) -> green (139, 0, rc=0); coverage triples 153 -> 154; no synthetic fixture needed
w0a_rolemap_validation.guard_limits: SUPERSET test -- catches UNDER-population only. Role CORRECTNESS (POPULATE vs READ) remains UNMECHANIZED.

Distinct from default_value/Check 20, which checks VALUES not gating STATE. Fixtures 14/17 ready.
w1_taxonomy_reconciliation.other_bucket: R55's 60 out-of-taxonomy claims WERE NEVER RECORDED -- they survive as one integer in one table cell. The single most valuable bottom-up signal in project history was destroyed at aggregation time. PROCESS FIX: depth audits must emit a per-claim ledger with the class tag, or the `other` bucket is unrecoverable by construction.
w1_taxonomy_reconciliation.best_build_target: The lone surviving `other` finding (module_56.md:1139 'Depends on: Modules 52, 53, 51'; truth includes 58) lands on a DOCUMENTED gap in Check 35's own LIMITS: it evaluates the DOWNSTREAM direction only and explicitly excludes upstream 'Depends on: N modules' claims. summary.design: Open-ended taxonomy (auditors given severity tiers but NOT the bug-class list, fixing R55's imposed-taxonomy flaw). Auditors barred from reading scripts/check_*.py or the role map -- auditing from inside the instrument's frame is the error under study. Adversarial refuter per module.

summary.comparison: R55: 28/498 = 5.6% on the 3 MOST-audited hubs. Criticals 7 vs 1. NOT A CLEAN COMPARISON: R55 imposed its taxonomy, R58 was open-ended, so an unknown share of the delta is method not corpus. The Critical ratio is the more robust signal (shared triggers).

qa_smoke_test.caveat: A QA mean over ~4-7 questions structurally CANNOT detect a 5-8% claim-level defect rate. Tonight it scored 8.375 on the same corpus where the depth arm found 46/595. It is a product smoke test and licenses NOTHING about corpus cleanliness. Quoting it as such IS the 9.52 error.

mutation_survival.verdict: FAILS by 10x. '0 findings' is still NOT licensed as 'clean'.

dead_to_test.caveat: DENOMINATORS DIFFER (164 -> 231) because the retrofit ADDED covered helper functions, inflating the denominator with passing code. Absolute dead count 67 -> 53.

the_grade.retrofit_denominator: 9 of 9 named extractors -- the number that makes the dead-to-test delta interpretable
the_grade.key_negative_result: Mutation survival barely moved (54.9% -> 51.2%) despite retrofitting all 9 extractors. dead-to-test asks 'is the function called at all' (a neuter probe is the crudest test); mutation asks 'would a one-character predicate change be caught'. The retrofit fixed the first and barely touched the second. check_consumer_attribution is now the WORST checker (74.8%) despite being retrofitted first, because its large scan_* functions remain uncovered. The instruments are BETTER, NOT FIXED.
the_grade.expiry: Expires on the next /sync touching module .gms files. Coverage/scope are properties of a code snapshot (develop @ 0d7ebeb90).

rounds.structural_finding: Both auditors converged INDEPENDENTLY on the same signal: the machine-checkable surface is clean and defects have MIGRATED to layers checkers cannot parse. M11: all 32 variable names, 32 citations, 27 module attributions, both formulas correct -- every defect in prose summaries or testing prescriptions. M29: all 16 formulas, ~20 defaults, 11/11 cross-module citations correct -- failures in presolve bounds, macro semantics, interface sets-vs-rows, and the doc's self-description. M29's auditor states the consequence: an audit reading equations.gms + declarations.gms + input.gms and scoring per-claim would find ZERO of the Criticals and would be justified writing the clean-bill banner. This is a MECHANISM for how 9.52 happened, not a restatement of it.

mechanized.check_38_cfg_gams_wiring: A REAL MAgPIE BUG CLASS. 3 of 312 scalar cfg$gms$ keys name a GAMS identifier that does not exist -- set them and nothing happens, silently: default.cfg:673 s21_trade_bal_damper, :1590 s52_plantation_threshold, :2197 s70_feed_subst_functional_form (real name s70_subst_functional_form; the sigmoid feed-substitution fader is UNREACHABLE from the config surface). Nothing else can catch it: check_config validates cfg against default.cfg itself; manipulateConfig is a regex sub with no existence check; GAMS never sees the key. PARENT-REPO BUGS -- reported, NOT fixed. Advisory.
mechanized.check_39_fenced_identifiers: Closes the gap that let the M29 Critical live for months under green gates: Check 14 (GATING) extracts only INLINE-BACKTICKED identifiers, so a phantom drawn in an ASCII diagram is invisible. Replay-validated against the real pre-fix module_29.md. Immediately found 2 MORE real phantoms (vm_demand, vm_import in module_17.md's dependency diagram, with module attributions, neither existing) in a module this round never audited. FP rate measured 32/32 -> 2 real + 1 suppressible BEFORE shipping (the Check 23 abort criterion did not fire). Advisory.

rounds.self_correction: I computed the target-selection degree centrality from the role map's OWN --dump-rolemap output, which lists a module's own declared variable in its read_by set. Every module double-counted self-references. Briefed M11 as 'degree 35, rank #2, consumes 33'; truth is degree 33, rank #1, produces 1 (vm_cost_glo), reads 32. M32 29/#2 not 40/#1; M29 19/#4 not 24; M70 16/#8 not 25. ROOT CAUSE: I used the instrument to select the targets and inherited its counting convention unexamined -- the exact error this round exists to catch, committed while designing the round. The auditors were barred from the instruments' frame; I did not hold myself to the same rule. Caught only because the M11 auditor tried to reproduce the figure and could not. Selection survives (M11 is MORE central than briefed); M70's inclusion at rank #8 was justified by an inflated number.
open_for_mike: GATING DECISION: 4 new advisory checks now exist (36/37 from R57, 38/39 tonight). None wired to gating.
PUSH APPROVAL: branch r57-rolemap-guard, 13 commits, nothing pushed.
3 MAgPIE parent-repo bugs: the dead cfg keys (Check 38). Not ours to fix.
39 surviving Major/Minor findings documented but unfixed.
The 3 unadjudicated dependent-count findings + Module_Dependencies.md twins: untouched per guardrail.

QA found 6 latent doc bugs in module_59.md (1 Critical: :1034 omits BOTH M45 and M52 from the dependency set). M59 was NOT audited tonight -- independent evidence the corpus has defects beyond the 3 hubs.

corrections.what: The 'left for review' claim was stale; the 39 Major/Minor findings were applied the same morning.

rounds.scope: Two arms, SCORED AND REPORTED SEPARATELY. Arm A = fix-verification on the regions R58's fix agents rewrote hours earlier. Arm B = fresh probes on central modules that have NEVER had a claim-level depth audit. Plus 2 regression anchors.
rounds.licensing_caveat: THE ROUND MEAN LICENSES NOTHING ABOUT CORPUS CLEANLINESS. R58's 4-question QA arm scored 8.375 on the same corpus where its depth arm found 46 confirmed defects in 595 claims (7.7%). A QA mean over ~12 questions STRUCTURALLY CANNOT detect a 5-8% claim-level defect rate. Power was raised 5->12 for R59; that narrows the per-arm interval, it does NOT escape this. What R59 CAN license: (a) whether R58's fixes broke something or left a Critical standing; (b) first claim-level signal on never-depth-audited hubs.
rounds.pooling_prohibition: Arm A and Arm B means are NEVER pooled. Arm A measures 'did the fix hold', NOT capability -- its docs were rewritten hours before the probe. A pooled mean would be meaningless and would repeat the 9.52 error. The collector (audit/archive/rounds/round59_split/collect_scores.py) has no code path that pools them.

headline_finding.claim: The answer scores barely separate the two arms (8.4 vs 7.6, well inside noise at n=5/arm). The LATENT doc-bug count separates them 3 vs 11.
headline_finding.why_it_matters: Latent bugs are exactly the case where the answerer was right ANYWAY -- invisible to the score, surfaced only because rubric §1.5 forces the auditor to check the doc the answer leaned on. A QA mean would have rated these two arms roughly equivalent. The doc layer underneath them is not.
headline_finding.CONFOUND: Arm B's auditors received MORE targeted 'adjudicate this specific doc claim' hints than Arm A's (B1 got four; B3 and B4 were each asked to adjudicate a named contradiction). An auditor told to adjudicate finds more latent bugs. AN UNKNOWN SHARE OF THE 3-vs-11 GAP IS METHOD, NOT CORPUS -- the same confound R58 flagged between R55's imposed taxonomy and its own open-ended lens, reproduced here. n=5/arm, no randomisation, different modules. Treat 3-vs-11 as a LEAD for a properly matched round, NOT an established rate.
headline_finding.criticals: Both Criticals in the round are LATENT: module_70_notes.md falsely asserted 'M70 reads' vm_costs_additional_mon (in the very entry that exists to prevent M70/M71 misattribution); module_52.md never received R58's stockType correction. Zero Criticals in any ANSWER.

fixes_applied.method: Grouped by shared root cause rather than one patch per finding.
fixes_applied.fix_suggestion_correction_detail: The doc-error rule auto-mandates an edit keyed on the auditor's classification, so each was verified before editing (R44/R48 protocol). No root-cause tag was wrong this round, but TWO auditor-proposed FIXES would have introduced new errors: (1) the module_52.md fix cited config/default.cfg:1835 as the authority for the actualNoAcEst default -- that is the DEAD line from finding F-7 (missing cfg$gms$ prefix, never reaches GAMS); corrected to price_aug22/input.gms:90. (2) the module_28.md audit proposed changing 'acx >=150 years' to '~145+', inferred from the ac_gfad_to_ac mapping; the explicit GAMS source comment at 28_ageclass/oct24/preloop.gms:12 says '150 years or older', so the number was kept and only the real bug (init-vs-dynamics conflation) fixed.
fixes_applied.goal_condition_sweep: audit/archive/rounds/round59_split/goal_sweep.sh -- added because delegation narrows verification to each agent's assigned files. Positive-controlled BEFORE trusting its output (run while module_28/module_70_notes still held their bugs; the relevant probes fired). It caught one real miss no agent owned: cross_module/carbon_balance_conservation.md:101 still cited the dead config/default.cfg:1835. It also flagged module_28.md:609, which was verified CORRECT ({ac0,ac5,ac10,ac15} is right for a 20-year timestep) -- checking before editing avoided introducing an error.

rounds.gate: bash scripts/validate_consistency.sh -> 47 checks / 45 passed / 2 warnings / 0 errors / PASS. Identical to the pre-flight baseline. Two defects the fixes introduced were caught by running the gate AFTER the last edit: a bare-basename .gms citation (Check 25 error) and an attribution-prose phantom the I7a scope note created (Check 33 warning). Both fixed. The 2 surviving warnings are pre-existing; note that the dependent-counts warning fires on module_17.md:899 and module_56.md:1138/:1150, which PLAN §0.6 holds for Mike -- it ships by explicit instruction, not silent deferral.
findings_for_mike: F-1: probe_dedup_ledger.json is stale by ~6 rounds (last appended source round R52; R53-R58 never appended) -- Step 5c silently skipped again, the same failure the 2026-05-29 note records as FIXED. Also PLAN §3's dedup framing was off: module_70 is 48 not 51, and <=51 at R59 means ELIGIBLE, not off-limits. Round unaffected.

F-2: AGENT.md's high-centrality hub list is probably wrong (PLAN §6). FLAGGED ONLY per §0.4, not edited. R59 supports it: M35, absent from that list, is the #3 hub and produced the round's worst score.

F-3: RESOLVED PLAN §6b's open M11 delta in R58's favour. The role-map extractor counts 'NAME.scale(...) =' as POPULATION. vm_cost_transp is declared AND populated by M40; M11 only reads it and sets .scale (11_costs/default/scaling.gms:10). So R58's 1 produces + 32 reads is correct and the parameterization session's 2+31 is inflated. Degree 33 unaffected -> Arm B selection stands. M70's 19-vs-16 delta REMAINS OPEN (read-side, different mechanism). Independently corroborated: B4's auditor hit the same trap at 50_nr_soil_budget/.../scaling.gms:8. The extractor also feeds check_attribution_omissions.py, and producer/consumer sets are the R20 Critical anchor class. NOT FIXED.

F-4: partial blinding leak on B2 (answerer used archived round answers). Refined by B2's auditor: m_carbon_stock_ac IS documented, but only in module_29.md:249-261, off the routing path for an M35 question; only m_carbon_stock's body is absent from all primary docs.

F-5: NEW phantom class with ZERO gate coverage -- prose module-number attribution. module_29.md:193 invented a 'Module 33'. Gates pass green because the identifier is backticked and real; only the prose attribution is false. Corpus sweep with positive control: exactly one instance, now fixed. Two independent agents proposed the same mechanization (assert every 'Module NN' in prose resolves to a real modules/NN_*/); a one-off version is implemented in goal_sweep.sh probe 1. NOT shipped as a validator (new-validator work is frozen).

F-6: MAgPIE SOURCE-PROSE ambiguity that regenerates doc bugs: 50_nr_soil_budget/macceff_aug22/equations.gms:20 says inorganic fertilizer 'are a free variable', but declarations.gms:9-10 declares it under positive variables. Any doc written from the *' comments inherits the error; it had already reached cross_module/nitrogen_food_balance.md:104. Fixed AND inoculated with a note naming the trap.

F-7: MAgPIE PARENT-REPO BUG. c56_carbon_stock_pricing is UNREACHABLE FROM CONFIG. GAMS declares it (56_ghg_policy/price_aug22/input.gms:90) and uses it in the CO2 pricing equation (equations.gms:22), but config/default.cfg:1835 omits the cfg$gms$ prefix every sibling carries, so the assignment creates an ordinary R variable and never reaches GAMS. LATENT, not active: config value and GAMS default are both actualNoAcEst, so nothing miscomputes until someone tries to change it -- and this switch selects which carbon-stock slice is PRICED. Check 38 (shipped by R58 for this very bug class) structurally cannot see it: its regex enumerates keys that HAVE the prefix. R58's 3 findings were key-present/identifier-absent; this is the INVERSE subclass. Swept default.cfg: exactly one such line. NOT FIXED -- parent repo, magpiemodel is push-forbidden. For the PIK briefing alongside R58's three dead keys.

F-8: PLAN §0.5 (isolation:'worktree') and §7 (the gate) are IN TENSION. The harness creates the worktree INSIDE the repo (.claude/worktrees/) with a full 61-doc copy, and validate_consistency.sh globs recursively -- with one present the gate read 53 checks / 7 errors / FAIL, every error citing a worktree path. Worse, the worktree checks out ONLY magpie-agent, not the GAMS parent, so checks 17-20 FATAL and the gate can NEVER run clean inside one; fix agents must read GAMS truth from outside the worktree anyway. Working protocol adopted: edit in worktree -> merge -> git worktree remove --force + delete branch -> gate in MAIN tree only. RECOMMEND AMENDING §0.5.

F-9: LEAD, unverified. I4 falsified module_51.md:472's 'No feedback loops' absolute (emissions DO reach the M11 objective via vm_emissions_reg -> q56_emission_costs -> vm_emission_costs, non-zero default GHG price at config/default.cfg:1731). Six other docs carry the same absolute phrasing (module_09.md:881, module_11.md:1199, module_55.md:525, module_37.md:1260, module_36.md:590, module_57.md:664). Several are plausibly TRUE on structural grounds. NONE verified, NONE touched. Candidate lens for a future round; NOT a defect list.
method_notes: Answerers were BLINDED from round59_split/ and validation_rounds.json, so no probe could see which regions R58 rewrote or which arm it belonged to (one partial leak, F-4).
Auditors were BLINDED to arm membership, so severity judgments were not biased by knowing a doc had been rewritten hours earlier.
The G3 auditor read project/version_pins.json directly at audit time as the rubric requires; no hardcoded version was scored against.

INSTRUMENTATION GAP NOT CLOSED (honest limitation, not reconstructed after the fact): verifier_gap tagging -- labelling each answerer_confabulation bug with the verifiers.md MANDATE that should have caught it -- was not built into the auditor prompts. That is the designed escalation signal for 'machinery problem vs local gap'.
